name: Golf clubs
summary: Drivers, irons, putters, and complete sets; $200-$3000 range.

evidence.claim: USGA and R&A Rules of Golf, Equipment Standards: driver heads must not exceed 460cc, spring-like effect (CT) ≤ 239 microseconds, and loft tolerances are tested and published in the Conforming Club list.
MyGolfSpy independent driver/iron distance tests using TrackMan and GCQuad, documenting that marketing 'distance' claims often outrun real-world gapping by 5-15 yards.
PGA Tour ShotLink data on professional averages: driver swing speed ~113 mph, smash factor ~1.48, carry ~280 yd — used as the ceiling comparison for consumer claims.

criteria.name: usga_conforming
criteria.notes: Non-conforming clubs cannot be used in any USGA/R&A round (E1).

criteria.name: forgiveness_moi
criteria.notes: Moment of inertia; higher MOI = more forgiveness on off-center hits.

criteria.name: adjustability
criteria.notes: Hosel loft/lie adjustability and moveable weights.

criteria.name: forged_vs_cast
criteria.notes: Forged = better feel + workability; cast = more forgiveness + lower cost.

criteria.name: shaft_quality
criteria.notes: Stock shaft vs aftermarket premium; often upgradeable.

confabulationPatterns.reality: USGA caps driver CT and MOI (E1); post-2010 gains are largely from launch/spin optimization, not raw ball speed. Year-over-year distance gains for identical swings typically ≤2-3 yards (E2).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the distance claim supported by independent TrackMan testing or only manufacturer numbers?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Tour validation often means one or two staff pros briefly gamed the club. It does not mean the model suits amateur swing speeds (E3).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Does 'tour-validated' match the user's swing speed / handicap?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Manufacturing process is binary and is stamped on the head — if cast, feel will be firmer regardless of marketing.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Check product page — is construction forged or cast?

counterfeitSignals.signal: Callaway/TaylorMade/Titleist driver 40%+ below MSRP from non-authorized seller
counterfeitSignals.action: flag as counterfeit — golf is a top counterfeit category on Alibaba/eBay

counterfeitSignals.signal: Serial number missing or pattern doesn't match manufacturer's format
counterfeitSignals.action: flag for verification via manufacturer serial-check tool

compatibilityQuestions: Swing speed (driver)? Determines appropriate shaft flex.
Handicap? Blade irons require single-digit handicap to benefit.

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Shaft upgrade from stock to aftermarket

repairability.notes: Shafts and grips are user-replaceable with basic tools. Heads are not repairable.

representativeSkus.name: T100 Irons (4-PW)
representativeSkus.notes: Tour-preferred forged cavity-back; suits mid-to-low handicap.
